Summary: Verify real-time report refresh behaves correctly at an edge condition under Goals & Reporting
Preconditions: A Goals & Reporting campaign exists with real-time report refresh active and receiving live traffic.
Test Steps:
1. Put the system into an edge condition relevant to real-time report refresh (e.g. zero traffic, single-variation test, campaign expiring mid-session).
2. Exercise real-time report refresh under this condition.
3. Observe behavior and any reporting produced.
Test Data: Edge-condition dataset for real-time report refresh (e.g. 0 visitors, 1 variation, concurrent campaign edit).
Expected Result: real-time report refresh degrades gracefully with no crash, no data corruption, and an accurate (or clearly empty) report.

Summary: Verify snippet content security policy compatibility renders and functions consistently across browsers for SmartCode
Preconditions: A published SmartCode campaign with snippet content security policy compatibility active is accessible from a cross-browser test grid.
Test Steps:
1. Load the page with the active snippet content security policy compatibility on Safari (Mobile (Android)).
2. Verify variation/feature rendering matches the design across the target combination.
3. Verify functional interactions tied to snippet content security policy compatibility work identically to the baseline browser.
Test Data: Target combination: Safari on Mobile (Android).
Expected Result: snippet content security policy compatibility renders and functions identically to the baseline browser on Safari/Mobile (Android), with no visual or functional regressions.

Summary: Verify mobile push notification test boundary limits under Mobile App Testing

Summary: Verify statistical significance calculation enforces access control and input sanitization under A/B Testing
Preconditions: Two accounts exist with different permission levels; a A/B Testing campaign uses statistical significance calculation.
Test Steps:
1. As a lower-privilege user, attempt to view/edit statistical significance calculation configuration directly via API or deep link.
2. Attempt to inject a script/HTML payload into any free-text field related to statistical significance calculation.
3. Attempt to replay or tamper with a request related to statistical significance calculation.
Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

Test Steps:
1. Set partial factorial design to its documented minimum allowed value/size and save.
2. Set partial factorial design to its documented maximum allowed value/size and save.
3. Attempt to set partial factorial design one unit beyond the maximum and save.
Test Data: Min, max, and max+1 values for the partial factorial design configurable field.
Expected Result: Min and max values are accepted and function correctly; max+1 is rejected with a clear limit-exceeded message.

Summary: Verify traffic allocation change mid-test UI renders and responds correctly under A/B Testing
Preconditions: User is logged into the VWO dashboard on a supported screen resolution.
Test Steps:
1. Open the A/B Testing module and locate the traffic allocation change mid-test control.
2. Verify layout, labels, tooltips, and iconography render correctly.
3. Interact with the traffic allocation change mid-test control (click, drag, type) and verify immediate visual feedback.
4. Resize the browser window and re-verify layout responsiveness.
Test Data: N/A — visual/interaction verification only.
Expected Result: traffic allocation change mid-test UI renders per design spec at all supported resolutions with no layout breaks or unresponsive controls.

Summary: Verify mobile crash handling during test rejects invalid input under Mobile App Testing
Preconditions: User is logged into VWO account; a Mobile App Testing campaign exists in draft state.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Mobile App Testing module.
2. Attempt to configure mobile crash handling during test using invalid or malformed input (e.g. empty required field, wrong type).
3. Attempt to save the configuration.
4. Observe system response.
Test Data: Invalid/malformed input for mobile crash handling during test (empty, wrong format, out-of-range).
Expected Result: VWO blocks the save with a clear, actionable validation error and does not persist the invalid mobile crash handling during test configuration.

Summary: Verify funnel report date comparison renders and functions consistently across browsers for Funnel Analysis

Summary: Verify heatmap device/browser filter enforces access control and input sanitization under Heatmaps & Recordings
Preconditions: Two accounts exist with different permission levels; a Heatmaps & Recordings campaign uses heatmap device/browser filter.

1. As a lower-privilege user, attempt to view/edit heatmap device/browser filter configuration directly via API or deep link.
2. Attempt to inject a script/HTML payload into any free-text field related to heatmap device/browser filter.
3. Attempt to replay or tamper with a request related to heatmap device/browser filter.
Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

Summary: Verify dynamic text replacement REST/SDK API contract under Web Personalization
Preconditions: A valid API key/SDK token is provisioned for a Web Personalization campaign using dynamic text replacement.
Test Steps:
1. Call the API/SDK endpoint associated with dynamic text replacement with a valid, well-formed request.
2. Inspect the response status code, payload schema, and latency.
3. Repeat with a missing/invalid auth token.
4. Repeat with a malformed request body.
Test Data: Valid request payload; missing-auth request; malformed JSON payload for dynamic text replacement.
Expected Result: Valid requests return 200 with the documented schema; invalid auth returns 401; malformed payloads return 400 with a descriptive error.

1. Set pageview goal to its documented minimum allowed value/size and save.
2. Set pageview goal to its documented maximum allowed value/size and save.
3. Attempt to set pageview goal one unit beyond the maximum and save.
Test Data: Min, max, and max+1 values for the pageview goal configurable field.
Expected Result: Min and max values are accepted and function correctly; max+1 is rejected with a clear limit-exceeded message.

Summary: Verify server-side event batching rejects invalid input under Server-Side Testing
Preconditions: User is logged into VWO account; a Server-Side Testing campaign exists in draft state.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Server-Side Testing module.
2. Attempt to configure server-side event batching using invalid or malformed input (e.g. empty required field, wrong type).
3. Attempt to save the configuration.
4. Observe system response.
Test Data: Invalid/malformed input for server-side event batching (empty, wrong format, out-of-range).
Expected Result: VWO blocks the save with a clear, actionable validation error and does not persist the invalid server-side event batching configuration.

Summary: Verify funnel step reordering enforces access control and input sanitization under Funnel Analysis
Preconditions: Two accounts exist with different permission levels; a Funnel Analysis campaign uses funnel step reordering.
Test Steps:
1. As a lower-privilege user, attempt to view/edit funnel step reordering configuration directly via API or deep link.
2. Attempt to inject a script/HTML payload into any free-text field related to funnel step reordering.
3. Attempt to replay or tamper with a request related to funnel step reordering.
Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

1. Navigate to the Web Personalization module.
2. Attempt to configure returning visitor personalization using invalid or malformed input (e.g. empty required field, wrong type).
3. Attempt to save the configuration.
4. Observe system response.
Test Data: Invalid/malformed input for returning visitor personalization (empty, wrong format, out-of-range).
Expected Result: VWO blocks the save with a clear, actionable validation error and does not persist the invalid returning visitor personalization configuration.

Summary: Verify SDK retry/backoff behavior boundary limits under Server-Side Testing
Preconditions: A Server-Side Testing campaign exists with SDK retry/backoff behavior configurable within documented limits.
Test Steps:
1. Set SDK retry/backoff behavior to its documented minimum allowed value/size and save.
2. Set SDK retry/backoff behavior to its documented maximum allowed value/size and save.
3. Attempt to set SDK retry/backoff behavior one unit beyond the maximum and save.
Test Data: Min, max, and max+1 values for the SDK retry/backoff behavior configurable field.
Expected Result: Min and max values are accepted and function correctly; max+1 is rejected with a clear limit-exceeded message.

Summary: Verify statistical significance calculation behaves correctly at an edge condition under A/B Testing
Preconditions: A A/B Testing campaign exists with statistical significance calculation active and receiving live traffic.
Test Steps:
1. Put the system into an edge condition relevant to statistical significance calculation (e.g. zero traffic, single-variation test, campaign expiring mid-session).
2. Exercise statistical significance calculation under this condition.
3. Observe behavior and any reporting produced.
Test Data: Edge-condition dataset for statistical significance calculation (e.g. 0 visitors, 1 variation, concurrent campaign edit).
Expected Result: statistical significance calculation degrades gracefully with no crash, no data corruption, and an accurate (or clearly empty) report.

Summary: Verify flag toggle propagation latency enforces access control and input sanitization under Feature Flags & Rollouts
Preconditions: Two accounts exist with different permission levels; a Feature Flags & Rollouts campaign uses flag toggle propagation latency.

Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

1. Navigate to the Segmentation module in the VWO dashboard.
2. Configure segment size estimation with valid, expected settings.
3. Save the configuration and publish the campaign.
4. Trigger the flow that exercises segment size estimation as an end visitor would.
Test Data: Valid campaign config; standard visitor session (no special flags).
Expected Result: segment size estimation behaves exactly as configured with no errors, and the result is reflected correctly in VWO reporting.

Summary: Verify campaign frequency capping UI renders and responds correctly under Web Personalization
Preconditions: User is logged into the VWO dashboard on a supported screen resolution.
Test Steps:
1. Open the Web Personalization module and locate the campaign frequency capping control.
2. Verify layout, labels, tooltips, and iconography render correctly.
3. Interact with the campaign frequency capping control (click, drag, type) and verify immediate visual feedback.
4. Resize the browser window and re-verify layout responsiveness.
Test Data: N/A — visual/interaction verification only.
Expected Result: campaign frequency capping UI renders per design spec at all supported resolutions with no layout breaks or unresponsive controls.

Summary: Verify integration disconnect/cleanup REST/SDK API contract under Integrations
Preconditions: A valid API key/SDK token is provisioned for a Integrations campaign using integration disconnect/cleanup.
Test Steps:
1. Call the API/SDK endpoint associated with integration disconnect/cleanup with a valid, well-formed request.
2. Inspect the response status code, payload schema, and latency.
3. Repeat with a missing/invalid auth token.
4. Repeat with a malformed request body.
Test Data: Valid request payload; missing-auth request; malformed JSON payload for integration disconnect/cleanup.
Expected Result: Valid requests return 200 with the documented schema; invalid auth returns 401; malformed payloads return 400 with a descriptive error.

Summary: Verify click goal enforces access control and input sanitization under Goals & Reporting [High priority]
Preconditions: Two accounts exist with different permission levels; a Goals & Reporting campaign uses click goal.
Test Steps:
1. As a lower-privilege user, attempt to view/edit click goal configuration directly via API or deep link.
2. Attempt to inject a script/HTML payload into any free-text field related to click goal.
3. Attempt to replay or tamper with a request related to click goal.
Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

Summary: Verify behavioral trigger personalization performance under load for Web Personalization
Preconditions: A Web Personalization campaign using behavioral trigger personalization is configured on a staging environment with load-testing tooling available.
Test Steps:
1. Baseline single-user latency for behavioral trigger personalization.
2. Ramp concurrent simulated visitors to a representative peak load.
3. Measure p50/p95/p99 latency and error rate for behavioral trigger personalization throughout the ramp.
4. Verify SmartCode/API related to behavioral trigger personalization does not measurably degrade host page load time.
Test Data: Load profile: baseline 1 user, peak concurrent visitor count per capacity plan.
Expected Result: p95 latency stays within agreed SLA, error rate stays below threshold, and host page load impact stays under the documented budget.

1. Call the API/SDK endpoint associated with Segment.io integration with a valid, well-formed request.
2. Inspect the response status code, payload schema, and latency.
3. Repeat with a missing/invalid auth token.
4. Repeat with a malformed request body.
Test Data: Valid request payload; missing-auth request; malformed JSON payload for Segment.io integration.
Expected Result: Valid requests return 200 with the documented schema; invalid auth returns 401; malformed payloads return 400 with a descriptive error.

Summary: Verify combination preview mode renders and functions consistently across browsers for Multivariate Testing [Medium priority]
Preconditions: A published Multivariate Testing campaign with combination preview mode active is accessible from a cross-browser test grid.
Test Steps:
1. Load the page with the active combination preview mode on Edge (Mobile (Android)).
2. Verify variation/feature rendering matches the design across the target combination.
3. Verify functional interactions tied to combination preview mode work identically to the baseline browser.
Test Data: Target combination: Edge on Mobile (Android).
Expected Result: combination preview mode renders and functions identically to the baseline browser on Edge/Mobile (Android), with no visual or functional regressions.

Summary: Verify segment-wise report breakdown behaves correctly at an edge condition under Goals & Reporting [Low priority]
Preconditions: A Goals & Reporting campaign exists with segment-wise report breakdown active and receiving live traffic.
Test Steps:
1. Put the system into an edge condition relevant to segment-wise report breakdown (e.g. zero traffic, single-variation test, campaign expiring mid-session).
2. Exercise segment-wise report breakdown under this condition.
3. Observe behavior and any reporting produced.
Test Data: Edge-condition dataset for segment-wise report breakdown (e.g. 0 visitors, 1 variation, concurrent campaign edit).
Expected Result: segment-wise report breakdown degrades gracefully with no crash, no data corruption, and an accurate (or clearly empty) report.

1. Put the system into an edge condition relevant to full factorial design (e.g. zero traffic, single-variation test, campaign expiring mid-session).
2. Exercise full factorial design under this condition.
3. Observe behavior and any reporting produced.
Test Data: Edge-condition dataset for full factorial design (e.g. 0 visitors, 1 variation, concurrent campaign edit).
Expected Result: full factorial design degrades gracefully with no crash, no data corruption, and an accurate (or clearly empty) report.

Summary: Verify SDK initialization timeout REST/SDK API contract under Server-Side Testing
Preconditions: A valid API key/SDK token is provisioned for a Server-Side Testing campaign using SDK initialization timeout.
Test Steps:
1. Call the API/SDK endpoint associated with SDK initialization timeout with a valid, well-formed request.
2. Inspect the response status code, payload schema, and latency.
3. Repeat with a missing/invalid auth token.
4. Repeat with a malformed request body.
Test Data: Valid request payload; missing-auth request; malformed JSON payload for SDK initialization timeout.
Expected Result: Valid requests return 200 with the documented schema; invalid auth returns 401; malformed payloads return 400 with a descriptive error.

Summary: Verify snippet caching behavior enforces access control and input sanitization under SmartCode
Preconditions: Two accounts exist with different permission levels; a SmartCode campaign uses snippet caching behavior.
Test Steps:
1. As a lower-privilege user, attempt to view/edit snippet caching behavior configuration directly via API or deep link.
2. Attempt to inject a script/HTML payload into any free-text field related to snippet caching behavior.
3. Attempt to replay or tamper with a request related to snippet caching behavior.
Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

1. Baseline single-user latency for personalization preview by segment.
2. Ramp concurrent simulated visitors to a representative peak load.
3. Measure p50/p95/p99 latency and error rate for personalization preview by segment throughout the ramp.
4. Verify SmartCode/API related to personalization preview by segment does not measurably degrade host page load time.
Test Data: Load profile: baseline 1 user, peak concurrent visitor count per capacity plan.
Expected Result: p95 latency stays within agreed SLA, error rate stays below threshold, and host page load impact stays under the documented budget.

Summary: Verify returning visitor personalization renders and functions consistently across browsers for Web Personalization
Preconditions: A published Web Personalization campaign with returning visitor personalization active is accessible from a cross-browser test grid.
Test Steps:
1. Load the page with the active returning visitor personalization on Chrome (Mobile (iOS)).
2. Verify variation/feature rendering matches the design across the target combination.
3. Verify functional interactions tied to returning visitor personalization work identically to the baseline browser.
Test Data: Target combination: Chrome on Mobile (iOS).
Expected Result: returning visitor personalization renders and functions identically to the baseline browser on Chrome/Mobile (iOS), with no visual or functional regressions.

Summary: Verify funnel step reordering enforces access control and input sanitization under Funnel Analysis [High priority]
Preconditions: Two accounts exist with different permission levels; a Funnel Analysis campaign uses funnel step reordering.
Test Steps:
1. As a lower-privilege user, attempt to view/edit funnel step reordering configuration directly via API or deep link.
2. Attempt to inject a script/HTML payload into any free-text field related to funnel step reordering.
3. Attempt to replay or tamper with a request related to funnel step reordering.
Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

Summary: Verify recording search/filter rejects invalid input under Heatmaps & Recordings [High priority]
Preconditions: User is logged into VWO account; a Heatmaps & Recordings campaign exists in draft state.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Heatmaps & Recordings module.
2. Attempt to configure recording search/filter using invalid or malformed input (e.g. empty required field, wrong type).
3. Attempt to save the configuration.
4. Observe system response.
Test Data: Invalid/malformed input for recording search/filter (empty, wrong format, out-of-range).
Expected Result: VWO blocks the save with a clear, actionable validation error and does not persist the invalid recording search/filter configuration.

Summary: Verify flag default value fallback behaves correctly at an edge condition under Feature Flags & Rollouts
Preconditions: A Feature Flags & Rollouts campaign exists with flag default value fallback active and receiving live traffic.
Test Steps:
1. Put the system into an edge condition relevant to flag default value fallback (e.g. zero traffic, single-variation test, campaign expiring mid-session).
2. Exercise flag default value fallback under this condition.
3. Observe behavior and any reporting produced.
Test Data: Edge-condition dataset for flag default value fallback (e.g. 0 visitors, 1 variation, concurrent campaign edit).
Expected Result: flag default value fallback degrades gracefully with no crash, no data corruption, and an accurate (or clearly empty) report.

1. Put the system into an edge condition relevant to Slack notification integration (e.g. zero traffic, single-variation test, campaign expiring mid-session).
2. Exercise Slack notification integration under this condition.
3. Observe behavior and any reporting produced.
Test Data: Edge-condition dataset for Slack notification integration (e.g. 0 visitors, 1 variation, concurrent campaign edit).
Expected Result: Slack notification integration degrades gracefully with no crash, no data corruption, and an accurate (or clearly empty) report.

Summary: Verify first-time vs returning visitor segment enforces access control and input sanitization under Segmentation [High priority]
Preconditions: Two accounts exist with different permission levels; a Segmentation campaign uses first-time vs returning visitor segment.
Test Steps:
1. As a lower-privilege user, attempt to view/edit first-time vs returning visitor segment configuration directly via API or deep link.
2. Attempt to inject a script/HTML payload into any free-text field related to first-time vs returning visitor segment.
3. Attempt to replay or tamper with a request related to first-time vs returning visitor segment.
Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

1. Set app version targeting to its documented minimum allowed value/size and save.
2. Set app version targeting to its documented maximum allowed value/size and save.
3. Attempt to set app version targeting one unit beyond the maximum and save.
Test Data: Min, max, and max+1 values for the app version targeting configurable field.
Expected Result: Min and max values are accepted and function correctly; max+1 is rejected with a clear limit-exceeded message.

Summary: Verify returning visitor personalization works correctly under Web Personalization [Highest priority]
Preconditions: User is logged into VWO account; a live Web Personalization campaign exists with returning visitor personalization configured.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Web Personalization module in the VWO dashboard.
2. Configure returning visitor personalization with valid, expected settings.
3. Save the configuration and publish the campaign.
4. Trigger the flow that exercises returning visitor personalization as an end visitor would.
Test Data: Valid campaign config; standard visitor session (no special flags).
Expected Result: returning visitor personalization behaves exactly as configured with no errors, and the result is reflected correctly in VWO reporting.

Summary: Verify segment size estimation behaves correctly at an edge condition under Segmentation [High priority]
Preconditions: A Segmentation campaign exists with segment size estimation active and receiving live traffic.
Test Steps:
1. Put the system into an edge condition relevant to segment size estimation (e.g. zero traffic, single-variation test, campaign expiring mid-session).
2. Exercise segment size estimation under this condition.
3. Observe behavior and any reporting produced.
Test Data: Edge-condition dataset for segment size estimation (e.g. 0 visitors, 1 variation, concurrent campaign edit).
Expected Result: segment size estimation degrades gracefully with no crash, no data corruption, and an accurate (or clearly empty) report.

1. Put the system into an edge condition relevant to custom domain snippet loading (e.g. zero traffic, single-variation test, campaign expiring mid-session).
2. Exercise custom domain snippet loading under this condition.
3. Observe behavior and any reporting produced.
Test Data: Edge-condition dataset for custom domain snippet loading (e.g. 0 visitors, 1 variation, concurrent campaign edit).
Expected Result: custom domain snippet loading degrades gracefully with no crash, no data corruption, and an accurate (or clearly empty) report.

Summary: Verify click goal works correctly under Goals & Reporting [High priority]
Preconditions: User is logged into VWO account; a live Goals & Reporting campaign exists with click goal configured.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Goals & Reporting module in the VWO dashboard.
2. Configure click goal with valid, expected settings.
3. Save the configuration and publish the campaign.
4. Trigger the flow that exercises click goal as an end visitor would.
Test Data: Valid campaign config; standard visitor session (no special flags).
Expected Result: click goal behaves exactly as configured with no errors, and the result is reflected correctly in VWO reporting.

Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

1. Call the API/SDK endpoint associated with scroll depth heatmap with a valid, well-formed request.
2. Inspect the response status code, payload schema, and latency.
3. Repeat with a missing/invalid auth token.
4. Repeat with a malformed request body.
Test Data: Valid request payload; missing-auth request; malformed JSON payload for scroll depth heatmap.
Expected Result: Valid requests return 200 with the documented schema; invalid auth returns 401; malformed payloads return 400 with a descriptive error.

Summary: Verify personalization preview by segment enforces access control and input sanitization under Web Personalization [Low priority]
Preconditions: Two accounts exist with different permission levels; a Web Personalization campaign uses personalization preview by segment.
Test Steps:
1. As a lower-privilege user, attempt to view/edit personalization preview by segment configuration directly via API or deep link.
2. Attempt to inject a script/HTML payload into any free-text field related to personalization preview by segment.
3. Attempt to replay or tamper with a request related to personalization preview by segment.
Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

Test Steps:
1. As a lower-privilege user, attempt to view/edit URL redirect mapping configuration directly via API or deep link.
2. Attempt to inject a script/HTML payload into any free-text field related to URL redirect mapping.
3. Attempt to replay or tamper with a request related to URL redirect mapping.
Test Data: Low-privilege session token; XSS payload string; tampered/replayed request.
Expected Result: Unauthorized access is denied (403), injected payloads are sanitized/escaped and never executed, and tampered requests are rejected.

Summary: Verify single-page-app snippet re-init performance under load for SmartCode [Medium priority]
Preconditions: A SmartCode campaign using single-page-app snippet re-init is configured on a staging environment with load-testing tooling available.
Test Steps:
1. Baseline single-user latency for single-page-app snippet re-init.
2. Ramp concurrent simulated visitors to a representative peak load.
3. Measure p50/p95/p99 latency and error rate for single-page-app snippet re-init throughout the ramp.
4. Verify SmartCode/API related to single-page-app snippet re-init does not measurably degrade host page load time.
Test Data: Load profile: baseline 1 user, peak concurrent visitor count per capacity plan.
Expected Result: p95 latency stays within agreed SLA, error rate stays below threshold, and host page load impact stays under the documented budget.

Summary: Verify recording privacy masking REST/SDK API contract under Heatmaps & Recordings [High priority]
Preconditions: A valid API key/SDK token is provisioned for a Heatmaps & Recordings campaign using recording privacy masking.
Test Steps:
1. Call the API/SDK endpoint associated with recording privacy masking with a valid, well-formed request.
2. Inspect the response status code, payload schema, and latency.
3. Repeat with a missing/invalid auth token.
4. Repeat with a malformed request body.
Test Data: Valid request payload; missing-auth request; malformed JSON payload for recording privacy masking.
Expected Result: Valid requests return 200 with the documented schema; invalid auth returns 401; malformed payloads return 400 with a descriptive error.

Summary: Verify editor autosave behaves correctly at an edge condition under Visual Editor [Low priority]
Preconditions: A Visual Editor campaign exists with editor autosave active and receiving live traffic.
Test Steps:
1. Put the system into an edge condition relevant to editor autosave (e.g. zero traffic, single-variation test, campaign expiring mid-session).
2. Exercise editor autosave under this condition.
3. Observe behavior and any reporting produced.
Test Data: Edge-condition dataset for editor autosave (e.g. 0 visitors, 1 variation, concurrent campaign edit).
Expected Result: editor autosave degrades gracefully with no crash, no data corruption, and an accurate (or clearly empty) report.

Summary: Verify mobile SDK update compatibility boundary limits under Mobile App Testing [Highest priority]
Preconditions: A Mobile App Testing campaign exists with mobile SDK update compatibility configurable within documented limits.
Test Steps:
1. Set mobile SDK update compatibility to its documented minimum allowed value/size and save.
2. Set mobile SDK update compatibility to its documented maximum allowed value/size and save.
3. Attempt to set mobile SDK update compatibility one unit beyond the maximum and save.
Test Data: Min, max, and max+1 values for the mobile SDK update compatibility configurable field.
Expected Result: Min and max values are accepted and function correctly; max+1 is rejected with a clear limit-exceeded message.

1. Call the API/SDK endpoint associated with scroll depth heatmap with a valid, well-formed request.
2. Inspect the response status code, payload schema, and latency.
3. Repeat with a missing/invalid auth token.
4. Repeat with a malformed request body.
Test Data: Valid request payload; missing-auth request; malformed JSON payload for scroll depth heatmap.
Expected Result: Valid requests return 200 with the documented schema; invalid auth returns 401; malformed payloads return 400 with a descriptive error.

Summary: Verify returning visitor personalization performance under load for Web Personalization [High priority]
Preconditions: A Web Personalization campaign using returning visitor personalization is configured on a staging environment with load-testing tooling available.
Test Steps:
1. Baseline single-user latency for returning visitor personalization.
2. Ramp concurrent simulated visitors to a representative peak load.
3. Measure p50/p95/p99 latency and error rate for returning visitor personalization throughout the ramp.
4. Verify SmartCode/API related to returning visitor personalization does not measurably degrade host page load time.
Test Data: Load profile: baseline 1 user, peak concurrent visitor count per capacity plan.
Expected Result: p95 latency stays within agreed SLA, error rate stays below threshold, and host page load impact stays under the documented budget.

Expected Result: Min and max values are accepted and function correctly; max+1 is rejected with a clear limit-exceeded message.
